key_insight: Ammonia catalyst discovery is reframed as a multi-intermediate reaction-network problem, and only ten-million-scale generative search reveals full compatibility across N*, NH*, NNH*, and HNNH*.
engineering_relevance: This enables automated discovery of catalysts that work across competing dissociative and associative pathways, expanding the search beyond conventional Fe/Ru motifs to new candidate families.
method: Adsorbate-specific generative Transformers explore ~15 million configurations per intermediate.
Structural compression and machine-learning potentials rapidly score candidate materials.
Overlap analysis identifies sparse regions where all four intermediates are jointly compatible.
evidence_signal: The pipeline produced 279 highly potential target materials, and representative DFT validation confirmed pathway-dependent leads such as Fe-V for dissociation and Al-Pd-Zr for associative stabilization.
watch_item: The strongest multi-intermediate compatibility emerges only at very large generative scales, so smaller searches may miss valid catalysts and ML-potential screening still requires targeted DFT validation.
paper_title: Reaction-Network-Level Discovery of Ammonia Synthesis Catalysts via Ten-Million-Scale Generative Exploration
